seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with Smolagents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with Smolagents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with Smolagents enables lightweight, code-driven automation for intent detection, draft generation, and approval workflows. However, its simplicity can lead to risks like unsafe code execution, PII leaks, and inappropriate content in high-stakes email handling. Vex guardrails ensure reliability by verifying outputs against critical criteria like confidentiality and tone before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with Smolagents
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant uses Smolagents' code agents for modular task execution: an intent detector classifies incoming emails, a draft generator creates responses with attachment handling, and an approval agent validates before sending. Tools interface with email APIs (e.g., Gmail) while agents chain reasoning steps for multi-turn workflows. Vex wraps the agent's run() method to verify outputs at each stage.

Key components include a router agent dispatching to specialized sub-agents, PII scanners in tools, and approval gates. This minimizes latency while maintaining safety through verifiable code generation patterns inherent to Smolagents.

risks.name: PII Exposure in Drafts
risks.description: Agents may include sensitive data like SSNs or addresses in generated email responses
risks.mitigation: Vex PII filtering policy scans all drafts before approval

risks.name: Unsafe Code Generation
risks.description: Smolagents code agents can generate exec() calls or file writes during email processing
risks.mitigation: Wrap agent.run() with Vex sandbox verification

risks.name: Inappropriate Tone
risks.description: Multi-step reasoning can compound to generate offensive or unprofessional language
risks.mitigation: Tone analysis policy in Vex verifies sentiment scores

risks.name: Invalid Recipients
risks.description: Tool misinterpretation leads to emails sent to wrong or spoofed addresses
risks.mitigation: Recipient validation policy cross-checks domains and formats

implementation.title: Intent Detection Agent
implementation.description: Classifies email intent using Smolagents code agent with LLM reasoning
implementation.code:
from smolagents import CodeAgent, LLM

llm = LLM(model='gpt-4o-mini')
intent_agent = CodeAgent(
    llm=llm,
    tools=[],
    instructions="""Analyze email content and return intent: 'reply', 'forward', 'archive', 'delete'. 
    Output ONLY valid intent as JSON: {'intent': 'reply'}"""
)

def detect_intent(email_content: str):
    result = intent_agent.run(email_content)
    return result.intent

implementation.title: Draft Generation with Attachments
implementation.description: Generates response drafts while safely handling attachments via tools

implementation.title: Email Router Agent
implementation.description: Routes tasks to appropriate sub-agents based on detected intent
implementation.code:
router_agent = CodeAgent(
    llm=llm,
    instructions="""Route email tasks:
    - 'reply' -> draft_agent
    - 'forward' -> forward_agent
    - else -> archive
    Return agent_name to call."""
)

def route_email(email_data: dict):
    intent = detect_intent(email_data['content'])
    route = router_agent.run({'intent': intent, 'email': email_data})
    return route.agent_name

implementation.title: Send Approval Workflow
implementation.description: Final validation before sending with human-in-loop option
